rules: Prefer an app factory and explicit extension wiring.
Keep HTTP transport thin; move business logic to services/use cases.
patterns: Repository pattern around persistence.
Background jobs for slow or retriable work via Celery.
security_checks: Validate input at the boundary and keep secrets out of config modules.
Require authn/authz before mutating endpoints.
refactor_rules: Split blueprint routes from application services.
Do not hide side effects inside request handlers.

rules: Keep settings modular and environment-specific.
Use service/use-case boundaries for non-trivial domain logic.
patterns: Repository-like adapters around ORM-heavy areas.
Separate read/write concerns for admin-heavy modules when useful.
security_checks: Keep CSRF/auth/session protections enabled by default.
refactor_rules: Extract cross-app workflows from views and models into services.

rules: Type annotate handlers and services consistently.
Keep async boundaries explicit and non-blocking.
patterns: Dependency injection for infrastructure adapters.
Background tasks only for small non-critical follow-ups; use a queue for durable jobs.
security_checks: Enforce auth scopes and validate request models.
refactor_rules: Move orchestration out of routers into use-case modules.

rules: Keep model code, retrieval code, and serving code in separate modules.
Version prompts, datasets, and evaluation criteria.
patterns: RAG pipelines with explicit chunking, embedding, retrieval, and grounding steps.
security_checks: Treat prompts, retrieved documents, and tool outputs as untrusted input.
refactor_rules: Isolate embedding providers and vector stores behind adapters.

rules: Keep configuration, controllers, and domain services separated.
Prefer constructor injection and clear transactional boundaries.
patterns: DDD aggregates with repositories only at aggregate roots.
Kafka-driven integration for decoupled workflows.
security_checks: Make security filters and role constraints explicit.
refactor_rules: Split overly broad services by bounded context.

rules: Keep ZUL views thin and route business use cases through application services.
Make session, desktop, and execution scoped state explicit and bounded.
patterns: MVVM with binder-backed view models for complex forms.
Selector composers only when event wiring is clearer than binder commands.
security_checks: Validate client-originated events and uploaded data on the server side.
Do not trust hidden fields or binder state as authorization proof.
refactor_rules: Move business rules out of composers, view models, and ZUL expressions into application services.

rules: Treat Lombok as boilerplate reduction, not as a replacement for explicit domain contracts.
Review generated equality, mutability, and constructor semantics intentionally.
patterns: Prefer targeted annotations over broad bundles when intent matters.
security_checks: Ensure generated toString or setters do not expose or weaken sensitive state.
refactor_rules: Replace handwritten boilerplate with Lombok only when tests preserve object semantics.

rules: Keep Maven plugin ownership, dependency convergence, and compiler settings explicit across modules.
Preserve current parent or reactor structure unless the task explicitly changes build organization.
patterns: Use dependency management or BOMs intentionally and separate unit versus integration test lifecycles clearly.
security_checks: Review repository, plugin, and build-extension additions as supply-chain changes.
refactor_rules: Separate build-lifecycle changes from business changes and prove compatibility with targeted tests.

rules: Make the minimum supported Java baseline explicit before using newer APIs or dependencies.
Keep compiler target, release, and toolchain settings aligned with the runtime support matrix.
patterns: Use adapter seams when newer modules must coexist with older runtime consumers.
security_checks: Review dependency and crypto support differences across the declared LTS baselines.
refactor_rules: Separate compatibility hardening from business changes.

rules: Prefer phased modernization behind stable seams instead of wide rewrites.
Keep backward-compatible contracts explicit while extracting APIs or modules.
patterns: Use strangler-style extraction and anti-corruption layers around legacy components.
security_checks: Re-evaluate auth, session, and exposure assumptions when moving legacy flows behind APIs.
refactor_rules: Separate modernization work from feature work and prove parity with targeted tests.

rules: Keep bootstrap, routing, and provider scope explicit in standalone Angular applications.
Use feature-local composition instead of rebuilding hidden module-style coupling.
patterns: Standalone feature entry components paired with route configuration and focused providers.
security_checks: Review guards, resolvers, and client-stored auth state as trust boundaries.
refactor_rules: Separate standalone wiring changes from business behavior changes and verify route/provider parity.

rules: Keep NgRx focused on durable application state and preserve clear reducer, selector, and effect boundaries.
Avoid using the store as a default home for transient UI flags or form-only state.
patterns: Selectors as read contracts and effects as async side-effect boundaries.
security_checks: Review API-calling effects and replayable client state for token or sensitive-data exposure.
refactor_rules: Separate state-model changes from unrelated component work and prove behavior through reducer/effect tests.

rules: Use composition root for DI; keep controllers thin.
Separate transport concerns from domain logic.
patterns: Mediator or service injection for command/query handling.
Options pattern for typed configuration.
security_checks: Apply [Authorize] explicitly; validate inputs at boundary.
refactor_rules: Move business logic from controllers to service classes.

rules: Keep DbContext scoped; use IEntityTypeConfiguration for entity config.
Separate read-optimized queries from write paths.
patterns: Repository pattern or thin data-access layer around DbContext.
No-tracking queries for read-only operations.
security_checks: Use parameterized queries; never concatenate user input into raw SQL.
refactor_rules: Extract EF-specific code behind data-access abstractions.

rules: One plugin class per logical operation; delegate to service/handler classes.
Use early-bound entity classes; avoid stringly-typed attribute access.
patterns: Service locator via IServiceProvider for tracing and organization service.
Custom API and Custom Action patterns for reusable server-side operations.
security_checks: Validate calling user context and execution depth to prevent recursion.
refactor_rules: Extract business logic from Execute method into dedicated service classes.

rules: Namespace-isolate all functions; use formContext from executionContext.
Use only supported Xrm SDK methods; avoid deprecated Xrm.Page.
patterns: Module pattern for encapsulation and namespace isolation.
Thin event handlers delegating to business logic functions.
security_checks: Do not expose secrets in client-side scripts; validate user roles before privileged actions.
refactor_rules: Separate Xrm SDK calls from pure business logic for testability.

rules: Use TypeScript for PCF controls; keep ControlFramework lifecycle methods focused.
Keep controls single-responsibility and decoupled from specific entity schemas.
patterns: ControlFramework init/updateView/destroy lifecycle.
Input/output property manifest for data binding.
security_checks: Do not expose sensitive configuration in control manifest or client properties.
refactor_rules: Extract rendering logic from lifecycle methods into composable functions.

rules: Use child flows for reusable automation fragments.
Use environment variables instead of hardcoded URLs or keys.
patterns: Trigger-action-condition flows with explicit error handling.
Custom connector with OpenAPI definition for external integrations.
security_checks: Review flow run-as context and connection ownership.
refactor_rules: Break monolithic flows into composable child flows.

rules: Preserve batch/job orchestration assumptions and dataset naming contracts.
Treat copybooks and JCL as contract artefacts, not incidental files.
patterns: Strangler integrations around stable batch or transaction boundaries.
security_checks: Trace RACF/ACF2/TSS dependencies before changing operational flows.
refactor_rules: Prefer wrapper/adaptor approaches over broad in-place rewrites.

rules: Keep domain and use cases independent from framework code.
Inbound and outbound adapters must depend inward only.
patterns: Use ports/adapters for persistence, messaging, and external APIs.
security_checks: Enforce authorization in use cases, not only in transport adapters.
refactor_rules: Move framework imports away from the core domain.

rules: Split services by bounded context and ownership, not by tables.
Make sync and async contracts explicit.
patterns: Event-driven integration for cross-service workflows where eventual consistency is acceptable.
security_checks: Authenticate service-to-service calls and track secret distribution.
refactor_rules: Avoid distributed monolith patterns created by shared persistence.

rules: Align modules with bounded contexts and ubiquitous language.
Protect aggregate invariants inside the domain layer.
patterns: Application services orchestrate aggregates, repositories, and domain events.
security_checks: Do not leak authorization rules into persistence-only layers.
refactor_rules: Rename technical modules to domain concepts when boundaries are clear.

rules: Define event contracts, ownership, and delivery guarantees explicitly.
Separate command and event paths when retries differ.
patterns: Outbox/inbox or idempotency patterns for reliable publication and consumption.
security_checks: Validate producer identity and protect sensitive payloads.
refactor_rules: Replace hidden side effects with explicit domain or integration events.

rules: Keep resource naming, identity, and environment separation explicit.
Prefer managed identities over embedded secrets.
patterns: IaC-driven provisioning with Terraform or Bicep and CI validation.
security_checks: Review Key Vault, RBAC, network exposure, and tenant boundaries.
refactor_rules: Isolate cloud SDK code behind adapters to keep domain logic portable.

rules: Keep account/environment boundaries and IAM ownership explicit.
Prefer managed identities/roles instead of long-lived keys.
patterns: Immutable infrastructure and repeatable deployments.
security_checks: Review IAM least privilege, secrets, and public exposure.
refactor_rules: Wrap AWS-specific integrations behind service adapters.

rules: Keep manifests/chart values environment-aware and reproducible.
Design readiness, liveness, and resource limits intentionally.
patterns: Twelve-factor configuration and explicit rollout/rollback strategy.
security_checks: Review RBAC, pod security, secret mounts, and network policies.
refactor_rules: Separate deployment topology concerns from application boot logic.

rules: Separate indexing, retrieval, and reranking responsibilities.
Version embedding models and index rebuild procedures.
patterns: Hybrid retrieval and metadata filtering for grounded answers.
security_checks: Review document access filtering and tenant isolation.
refactor_rules: Abstract vector store operations behind a repository/adapter.

rules: Make retrieval, context assembly, and generation steps observable and testable.
Cite or ground outputs against retrieved evidence.
patterns: Offline evaluation sets and online guardrails for retrieval quality.
security_checks: Treat retrieved content and tool outputs as untrusted.
refactor_rules: Keep prompt templates and retrieval policies versioned separately from app code.

rules: Use deterministic base images and explicit build stages.
Keep runtime images minimal and non-root where feasible.
patterns: Multi-stage builds with isolated dependency restore layers.
security_checks: Review secrets leakage in layers and image privilege settings.
refactor_rules: Move environment-specific values out of Dockerfiles into deployment config.

rules: Keep build, test, security, and deploy stages explicit and reproducible.
Fail fast on validation and security gates.
patterns: Promotion pipelines with immutable artifacts and environment approvals.
security_checks: Review secret scoping, runner trust, and supply-chain controls.
refactor_rules: Extract reusable pipeline logic into shared templates/actions.

rules: Keep modules small, composable, and environment-aware.
Pin providers and validate plans in CI.
patterns: Remote state with locking and workspace/account separation.
security_checks: Review secrets in variables, state exposure, and IAM grants.
refactor_rules: Move repeated resource sets into versioned modules.

rules: Map code and architecture changes against the relevant OWASP risk classes.
Prefer concrete mitigations over generic security statements.
patterns: Threat-model input validation, auth, secrets, and dependency risk in every change.
security_checks: Review injection, authz, secrets, SSRF, unsafe deserialization, and supply-chain surfaces.
refactor_rules: Extract reusable security controls into shared middleware/services.

rules: Treat all external input and generated output as untrusted.
Keep secret handling, authorization, and auditability explicit.
patterns: Centralized validation, least privilege, and safe defaults.
security_checks: Check authz, secrets, logging, error disclosure, and dependency provenance.
refactor_rules: Remove duplicated security decisions from edge handlers and centralize them.

rules: Security is a cross-cutting default, not an optional post-step.
Every generated change should include auth, secrets, validation, and logging considerations.
patterns: Default to least privilege, explicit validation, and observable failure modes.
security_checks: Review authn/authz, secret exposure, input validation, and dangerous deserialization.
refactor_rules: Consolidate scattered security checks into shared, testable controls.

rules: Emit logs, metrics, and traces aligned with service boundaries and critical paths.
Make failure, retry, and saturation visible.
patterns: Correlation IDs, structured logs, SLO-aware dashboards, and alerting.
security_checks: Do not leak secrets or sensitive payloads in telemetry.
refactor_rules: Replace ad-hoc prints with structured telemetry abstractions.

rules: Keep repository interfaces owned by the application/domain layer.
Hide persistence implementation details behind adapters.
patterns: Separate query models from aggregate mutation paths when complexity warrants it.
security_checks: Prevent authorization leakage by keeping data access guards explicit.
refactor_rules: Move ORM-specific code out of business services.

rules: Keep session lifecycle explicit and scoped to use cases/requests.
patterns: Repository/adaptor around ORM operations.
security_checks: Parameterize queries and protect migration credentials.
refactor_rules: Move model-to-domain translation out of handlers.

rules: Make cache, queue, and lock usage explicit; do not mix roles blindly.
patterns: Use TTLs, idempotency keys, and namespaced keys deliberately.
security_checks: Review auth, network exposure, and sensitive payload storage.
refactor_rules: Wrap direct client calls behind focused infrastructure services.

rules: Keep task payloads minimal and idempotent.
Separate orchestration tasks from domain logic.
patterns: Retry policies and dead-letter handling for unstable integrations.
security_checks: Review broker credentials and sensitive task payload handling.
refactor_rules: Move long-running work out of HTTP request paths.

rules: Make transactional boundaries and fetch strategies explicit.
patterns: Repository interfaces at aggregate/application boundaries.
security_checks: Avoid unsafe dynamic query composition.
refactor_rules: Keep entity mapping details out of service orchestration code.

rules: Define topic ownership, schema evolution, and retry semantics.
patterns: Outbox pattern and consumer idempotency.
security_checks: Review ACLs, credentials, and PII in events.
refactor_rules: Hide producer/consumer client specifics behind ports.

rules: Make schema ownership, migrations, and connection handling explicit.
patterns: Migrations in CI and adapter-based access boundaries.
security_checks: Review credential handling, TLS, and least-privilege roles.
refactor_rules: Move ad-hoc SQL scattered across handlers into persistence adapters.

rules: Separate HAL from application logic; keep ISRs minimal.
Design for deterministic memory usage; prefer static allocation.
patterns: HAL with register abstraction and driver interfaces.
State machines for protocol and device control logic.
security_checks: Validate all external input from serial, network, and sensors.
refactor_rules: Extract hardware register access behind abstraction layers.

rules: Always check return values of system calls and handle errors explicitly.
Pair every resource acquisition with a documented release path.
patterns: Callback-based event loops for I/O multiplexing.
Opaque pointer pattern for encapsulation in C.
security_checks: Drop unnecessary privileges early; validate all external input.
refactor_rules: Separate system-level operations from application logic through abstractions.

rules: Use RAII exclusively for resource management; no raw new/delete.
Prefer smart pointers; use unique_ptr for exclusive, shared_ptr only when genuinely shared.
patterns: Type-safe variants and optionals for nullable or polymorphic values.
Value semantics with move support for efficient data transfer.
security_checks: Use bounded containers; validate external input with strong typing.
refactor_rules: Replace raw pointers with smart pointers; replace C-style casts with named casts.

rules: Use RAII with static or pool-based allocation; avoid heap in real-time paths.
Restrict or disable exceptions and RTTI on constrained platforms.
patterns: CRTP for zero-cost polymorphism; static memory pools for bounded allocation.
security_checks: Validate sensor and communication input at system boundaries.
refactor_rules: Extract hardware register access behind type-safe abstractions.

rules: Separate business logic from UI; use Model-View patterns.
Keep QObject ownership hierarchy explicit; parent-child manages lifetime.
patterns: Signals and slots for decoupled communication.
Q_PROPERTY with NOTIFY signal for QML data binding.
security_checks: Validate user input from forms; sanitize QML loaded from external sources.
refactor_rules: Extract business logic from slots into testable service classes.

rules: Separate engine systems from game logic; design for frame-budget awareness.
Keep hot-path code cache-friendly with data-oriented design.
patterns: ECS for composable game objects; custom allocators for predictable allocation.
security_checks: Validate asset loading and deserialization from external files.
refactor_rules: Decouple rendering API specifics from game logic.
